source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure RDP is restricted to only IP address that should have this access.

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure RDP is restricted to only IP address that should have this access.
input_row.description: Any ports enable within Lightsail by default are open and exposed to the world. For SSH and RDP access you should identify which IP address need access.
input_row.rationale: Any ports enable within Lightsail by default are open and exposed to the world. This can result in outside traffic trying to access or even deny access to the Lightsail instances. Removing and adding approved IP address required for access.
input_row.audit: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Windows Instance you want to review. 5. Go to the Networking section. 6. Confirm that the RDP Port is restricted to an IP address Application Protocol Port or range / Code Restricted to RDP  TCP  3389   101.221.11.11 7. If RDP is needed and it is open to Any IPv4 address refer to the remediation below. From the Command Line: 1. Run aws lightsail get-instances aws lightsail get-instances --query "instances[*].name" This command will provide a list of Instance names. "WordPress-1", "Windows_Server_2019-1"  2. Run aws lightsail get-instance-port-states for any Windows instances listed aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name> This command will provide a list of available Ports for the Instance name. { "fromPort": 3389, "toPort": 3389, "protocol": "tcp", "state": "open", "cidrs": [ "0.0.0.0/0" ], "cidrListAliases": [] }, 3. Review the Port 22 settings and confirm that the only IP Addresses that should have access to the instance are listed in the cidrs as shown above. 4. If it is open to all ports (0.0.0.0/0) of there is an IP address listed that shouldn't have access refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Windows Instance you want to review. 5. Go to the Networking section. 6. Under IPv4 networking find the SSH rule as shown below. Application Protocol Port or range / Code Restricted to RDP  TCP  3389   Any IPv4 address 7. Click on the edit icon 8. Click on the check box next to Restrict to IP address 9. Under Source IP address (192.0.2.0) or range (192.0.2.0- 192.0.2.255 or 192.0.2.0/24) type the IP address' you want.  From the Command Line: 1. Run aws lightsail put-ins aws lightsail put-instance-public-ports --instance-name <instance_name> -- port-info fromPort=3389,protocol=TCP,toPort=3389,cidrs=110.111.221.100/32,110.111.221.2 02/32 This command will enter the IP addresses that should have access to the instances identified above in the Audit. 2. Run aws lightsail get-instance-port-states for the Windows instance to confirm the new setting. aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name> This command will provide a list of available Ports and show how the cidr value for Port 3389 is now set. "portStates": [ { "fromPort": 3389, "toPort": 3389, "protocol": "tcp", "state": "open", "cidrs": [ "110.111.221.100/32", "110.111.221.202/32" ], "cidrListAliases": [] } 3. Repeat the remediation below for all other Windows instances identified in the Audit. References: 1. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/lightsail/ind ex.html#cli-aws-lightsail

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

Based on the provided compliance control related to AWS Lightsail and RDP port restrictions, the audit can be automated. The audit field mentions specific AWS CLI commands like `aws lightsail get-instance-port-states`, which implies that the configurations can be retrieved programmatically. These commands access the network settings of Lightsail instances, allowing us to check if RDP ports are restricted to specific IP addresses.

Given these factors:

✓ The AWS Lightsail APIs (`boto3` and AWS CLI) can query this property.
✓ It is a standard AWS service property related to network rules (port access restrictions).
✓ The audit field mentions AWS CLI commands to verify the settings.
✓ It does not require SSH/RDP to instances nor involve OS-level checks; it relies on network access parameters.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lightsail_instance_rdp_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

To automate the compliance check, the following approach will be taken:

- **AWS CLI Command:** Use `aws lightsail get-instance-port-states` to retrieve network settings for each Windows Lightsail instance. This command lists all open ports and the CIDR blocks they permit.
  
- **AWS API/Boto3 Method:** Utilize the `boto3` Lightsail client to invoke `get_instance_port_states()` for each instance to achieve the same effect programmatically.

- **Enumerate Resources:** Enumerate all Windows instances using `aws lightsail get-instances` to gather the instance names.

- **Validate Configuration:** For each instance, check port 3389 (RDP) settings. Ensure the `cidrs` list does not include "0.0.0.0/0" and only specified, approved IPs are present. The acceptable state must restrict access to specific IPs rather than being open.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** The RDP port (3389) is restricted to defined CIDR blocks, not publicly accessible from "0.0.0.0/0".
  - **Fail:** The RDP port (3389) is open to "0.0.0.0/0" or includes unauthorized CIDRs.

This automated approach will ensure continuous compliance with the CIS benchmark by programmatically checking for IP restrictions on RDP ports across all relevant Lightsail instances.
